dealbreakers: fast-paced environment (soft skill — not evidenced in resume language)
recommendations: Add TypeScript explicitly to your skills and mention it in at least one bullet if you have used it.
Reframe React bullets to emphasise scalable or reusable component architecture.
Add a soft-skill signal in your summary indicating comfort with high-velocity delivery cycles.

missingKeywords.suggestedBullet: Thrives in fast-paced, agile delivery environments, consistently shipping features under tight timelines.

gapAnalysis.summaryChanges: Rewritten to mirror the JD's emphasis on React, TypeScript, Node.js, and scalable UI delivery, and to signal seniority appropriate for a Software Engineer IC role.

resume.summary: Full-stack software engineer with 5 years of experience designing and delivering scalable UI components and REST APIs using React, TypeScript, and Node.js. Proven ability to build maintainable frontend architectures and robust server-side services in fast-paced delivery environments. Combines a strong engineering foundation with a pragmatic, impact-focused approach to full-stack development.

coverLetter.subject: Application for Software Engineer
coverLetter.body:
With five years of full-stack web development experience centred on React and Node.js, I have spent the last several years at Tech Corp designing and delivering production-grade web applications and REST APIs that serve real users at scale. My work spans the full delivery lifecycle — from architecting reusable UI components to building and maintaining the Node.js APIs that power them.

The role you are hiring for maps closely to my day-to-day: building scalable UI components in React and shipping reliable APIs in Node.js. I have also been deepening my TypeScript proficiency, applying it to improve type safety and long-term maintainability across frontend codebases — exactly the kind of discipline that reduces bugs and speeds up team velocity in a fast-moving environment.

I am drawn to roles where the pace is high and the engineering bar is higher. I work well with ambiguity, move quickly without sacrificing quality, and care about writing code that the next engineer will thank you for rather than curse.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my background aligns with your team's needs. Thank you for your consideration.
